channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Iran war: Look beyond stocks to understand state of economy, experts say
item.description: Yields on 10-year US Treasury bills, a bellwether for inflation, are up 60 basis points since the start of the Iran war.

item.title: How a 64-team World Cup could transform football’s economics
item.description: Spain, Portugal and Morocco are the cohosts of the 2030 edition, but we await confirmation of 48 or 64 competitors.

item.title: India’s ‘cockroach’ protest movement halts marches after police violence
item.description: Leaders of the movement say they will continue campaigning but will not stage another march through New Delhi.

item.title: Hunger is falling but is still higher than pre-COVID levels: UN report
item.description: Global hunger has declined, yet millions of people are still going hungry.

item.title: Hamas’s new leader must bridge a movement divided over Iran
item.description: After a one-vote victory, Khalil al-Hayya must reconcile rival currents while preserving Hamas’s independence.

item.title: ‘Won’t back down’: Indian student protesters return after police crackdown
item.description: More than 100 students injured in police action against thousands of demonstrators during march on parliament.

item.title: Lebanese president set to meet Trump as Beirut seeks Israeli withdrawal
item.description: Aoun is in Washington, DC seeking US backing to end the fighting and secure an Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon.

item.title: Arizona’s primary elections: Which US races to watch and why they matter
item.description: From the governor’s race to Democratic divisions over Gaza, here’s what could shape Arizona elections on Tuesday.

item.title: Lebanon’s Joseph Aoun to meet Trump: What to expect
item.description: The Lebanese president is seeking Washington's support for his agenda, including diplomacy with Israel.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Who had the most goals and assists at the tournament?
item.description: FIFA’s biggest global showpiece saw 1,039 players from 48 nations play across 16 venues and score 308 goals.

item.title: Can AI systems make moral decisions in war?
item.description: As more militaries adopt AI technology into their kill chain, Al Jazeera asks if they are capable of moral judgement.

item.title: Andy Burnham to cut taxes on electricity bills, announces new UK cabinet
item.description: Burnham is attempting to act quickly ​to deliver on promises to tackle the UK's cost-of-living crisis.

item.title: Australian MP interrupts tough interview to yell at dog
item.description: Australian politician Barnaby Joyce interrupted a live TV interview to shout at his dog.

item.title: Huge fire at Ukraine shopping centre after Russian drone strike
item.description: A fire engulfed a shopping centre in Ukraine's Sumy after Russian drone strikes hit the building.

item.title: Argentina deny World Cup villains’ role after Samuel Jackson-led slants
item.description: Hollywood actor Samuel L Jackson was among those who criticised Argentina at World Cup, with racism among allegations.

item.title: Philippine Foreign Secretary says ASEAN must work together
item.description: Philippine Foreign Secretary says ASEAN must work together

item.title: Philippine Foreign Secretary says ASEAN must work together
item.description: Philippine Foreign Secretary says ASEAN must work together

item.title: Hormuz tankers on fire, as US, Iran continue attacks: What’s the latest?
item.description: The White House told Al Jazeera that the US would continue its attacks unless Trump decides otherwise.

item.title: Mamdani says he takes Netanyahu’s ICC arrest warrant ‘seriously’
item.description: Mamdani says he takes Netanyahu’s ICC arrest warrant ‘seriously’

item.title: Farage, Reform in ‘trouble’, as UK braces for crypto influence in politics
item.description: Farage's crisis after revelations of substantial crypto donations prompts broader political questions over donor clout.

item.title: Israeli forces open fire on children in Syrian village
item.description: Israeli forces open fire on children in Syrian village

item.title: Bangladesh face Australia Test might as Cummins, Hazlewood and Lyon return
item.description: Australia return to full strength for two-Test series against Bangladesh, as injured trio return to strengthen squad.

item.title: Moment terrifying floodwaters sweep through Afghan village
item.description: Moment terrifying floodwaters sweep through Afghan village

item.title: Five Palestinians killed in fire after Israeli attack in Gaza
item.description: An Israeli air attack on an apartment in Gaza City set the building on fire, killing five people.

item.title: World Cup: Messi thanks Argentina fans but ‘wound will take time to heal’
item.description: Argentina were beaten 1-0 in the final by Spain in what has been rumoured to be Lionel Messi's final World Cup game.
